subject: Cloud Computing
title: IaaS: Rent the Hardware
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on IaaS: Rent the Hardware.

hook.text: Need a server? Don't buy one. Rent one by the hour from a cloud provider. That's IaaS.

buildup.text: IaaS gives you virtual machines, storage, and networking. You manage everything above the hardware.

discovery.text: AWS EC2, Azure Virtual Machines, and Google Compute Engine are the big three IaaS offerings.

twist.text: IaaS gives maximum control but maximum work. You patch the OS, configure security, handle scaling.

climax.text: IaaS is ideal when you need full control — custom OS images, specific network setups, legacy apps.

punchline.text: IaaS: the cloud gives you a computer. The rest is up to you.

quiz.question: What does IaaS provide to the customer?
options: Virtual machines, storage, and networking — customer manages the rest
A fully managed application ready to use immediately
Only physical server space in a data center
